Think of the matrix as having layers of rings. You traverse each ring in order from outside in.
Try maintaining four boundaries: `top`, `bottom`, `left`, `right`. After traversing each side of the current layer, shrink the corresponding boundary.
Traverse: right across the top row → down the right column → left across the bottom row → up the left column. Then move all 4 boundaries inward and repeat.
Be careful about single row or column cases — when `top == bottom` or `left == right`, don't double-traverse.
